summary.final_report:
Analyst Closing Note

## Closing Note – youtubeaccount.com Investigation

**Case seed:** `http://youtubeaccount.com` | **Iterations completed:** 12 | **Stop reason:** Safety iteration limit reached (lateral_correlation phase)

---

**Suspicious Entrypoint and Strongest Signals**

The investigation opened on `http://youtubeaccount.com`, a URL that carries an unambiguous malicious verdict from VirusTotal. The domain name itself is a clear brand-impersonation attempt against YouTube, and the infrastructure quickly confirmed the suspicion. The URL redirects to `http://ww38.youtubeaccount.com/`, resolving to IP `185.53.177.29`, which is hosted by **Team Internet AG** (ASN 61969) — a provider frequently associated with parking and redirect infrastructure. A particularly strong signal is a **certificate mismatch**: the URL was observed using certificates issued to `blocxd.io`, `*.northwavepoint.com`, and `yfdnzf.com`, none of which have any legitimate relationship to a YouTube-branded domain. This divergence between the presented domain and the certificates in use strongly suggests shared or rotated infrastructure serving multiple campaigns simultaneously.

---

**Most Important Pivots and What They Yielded**

The two highest-value pivots drove the bulk of the investigation's evidence. First, expanding `http://ww38.youtubeaccount.com/` during seed characterization added 28 relations and 170 pieces of evidence, surfacing additional URL and hash observables that confirmed the redirect chain is active and instrumented. Second, and more significant, the URL `https://yfdnzf.com/?dn=youtubeaccount.com&pid=9po755g95` emerged during lateral correlation and proved to be the single most productive artifact in the case — yielding 32 relations and 201 pieces of evidence, including certificate, hash, URL, and detection-engine observables. The query parameter structure (`dn=youtubeaccount.com&pid=9po755g95`) is consistent with a **traffic distribution system (TDS) or affiliate redirect framework**, where `youtubeaccount.com` is registered as a destination and `9po755g95` likely identifies an affiliate or campaign ID. This pivot is the clearest link between the seed and a broader monetization or phishing delivery network.

---

**Recurring Lateral Families and Why They Matter**

Three domain families recurred across the investigation and together account for the majority of the correlated infrastructure. The **`youtubeaccount.com` family** (1 expansion, 28 relations, 170 evidence items) anchors the impersonation lure itself. The **`yfdnzf.com` family** appeared in two separate expansion passes (combined: 41 relations, 265 evidence items), reinforcing its role as a central redirect or TDS node rather than a one-off artifact — the fact that it surfaced independently more than once suggests it is structurally connected to multiple downstream destinations. calls.raw_response:
## Closing Note – youtubeaccount.com Investigation

**Case seed:** `http://youtubeaccount.com` | **Iterations completed:** 12 | **Stop reason:** Safety iteration limit reached (lateral_correlation phase)

---

**Suspicious Entrypoint and Strongest Signals**

The investigation opened on `http://youtubeaccount.com`, a URL that carries an unambiguous malicious verdict from VirusTotal. The domain name itself is a clear brand-impersonation attempt against YouTube, and the infrastructure quickly confirmed the suspicion. The URL redirects to `http://ww38.youtubeaccount.com/`, resolving to IP `185.53.177.29`, which is hosted by **Team Internet AG** (ASN 61969) — a provider frequently associated with parking and redirect infrastructure. A particularly strong signal is a **certificate mismatch**: the URL was observed using certificates issued to `blocxd.io`, `*.northwavepoint.com`, and `yfdnzf.com`, none of which have any legitimate relationship to a YouTube-branded domain. This divergence between the presented domain and the certificates in use strongly suggests shared or rotated infrastructure serving multiple campaigns simultaneously.

---

**Most Important Pivots and What They Yielded**

The two highest-value pivots drove the bulk of the investigation's evidence. First, expanding `http://ww38.youtubeaccount.com/` during seed characterization added 28 relations and 170 pieces of evidence, surfacing additional URL and hash observables that confirmed the redirect chain is active and instrumented. Second, and more significant, the URL `https://yfdnzf.com/?dn=youtubeaccount.com&pid=9po755g95` emerged during lateral correlation and proved to be the single most productive artifact in the case — yielding 32 relations and 201 pieces of evidence, including certificate, hash, URL, and detection-engine observables. The query parameter structure (`dn=youtubeaccount.com&pid=9po755g95`) is consistent with a **traffic distribution system (TDS) or affiliate redirect framework**, where `youtubeaccount.com` is registered as a destination and `9po755g95` likely identifies an affiliate or campaign ID. This pivot is the clearest link between the seed and a broader monetization or phishing delivery network.

---

**Recurring Lateral Families and Why They Matter**

Three domain families recurred across the investigation and together account for the majority of the correlated infrastructure. The **`youtubeaccount.com` family** (1 expansion, 28 relations, 170 evidence items) anchors the impersonation lure itself.
